title: Media Advisory | Advancing disability rights: United Nations Conference to mark historic progress while addressing critical implementation gaps
content:
MEDIA ADVISORY Advancing disability rights: United Nations Conference to mark historic progress while addressing critical implementation gaps Follow LIVE on UN Web TV WHAT: While the 20th anniversary of the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD) marks two decades of historic progress—including strengthened global laws, greater public visibility, and the integration of disability rights into the [...]

title: ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight
content:
The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is showing signs of progress – but significant challenges remain in testing, surveillance, vaccine development and building community trust.  Read Full Story on UN News

The post ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
content:
As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.  Read Full Story on UN News

The post Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
content:
Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the latest analysis by the leading UN-backed global food security platform. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: ‘You are not alone’: WHO chief vows to stand with DR Congo through Ebola outbreak
content:
Community trust will be decisive in bringing the rapidly evolving Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo under control, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Saturday, as health teams race to contain the emergency that has spread across multiple provinces and into neighbouring Uganda. Read Full Story on UN News

The post ‘You are not alone’: WHO chief vows to stand with DR Congo through Ebola outbreak appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
content:
Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths. Read Full Story on UN News

title: WHO chief calls for ceasefire amid DR Congo Ebola outbreak
content:
The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) headed to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) on Thursday as the country continues to combat a deadly resurgence of Ebola in its volatile eastern region where instability is rife.  Read Full Story on UN News

title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
content:
The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
content:
As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic. Read Full Story on UN News

The post International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
